refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency and safety compatibility. It avoids specifying how latency is achieved, maintaining solution neutrality. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety requirement without prescribing implementation details.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) is less than or equal to 150 ms, ensuring compliance with safety standards.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency data transfer
Design a real-time data compression and decompression mechanism to reduce transmission time
Establish a prioritized data scheduling system to ensure critical control commands are transmitted first
Incorporate local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Deploy an end-to-end latency monitoring and adaptive adjustment system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Establish a prioritized data scheduling system to ensure critical control commands are transmitted first

potential_sources: Interaction between data scheduling and communication protocol layers
Dependency on network conditions affecting scheduling effectiveness
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on logical prioritization of data which can be implemented independently of specific communication protocols or compression methods, supporting modularity and reducing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Prioritization logic correctly identifies critical control data without omission
Potential variability in network performance could still cause latency spikes
Local predictive control is not selected here, which may limit compensation for unexpected delays
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating local predictive control algorithms as a complementary DP to further mitigate latency impact and enhance safety under variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing remote control commands and their transmission scheduling

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives control commands and task priorities from higher-level systems

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends prioritized control commands over the communication network

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many unrelated functions, leading to high diffusion.
Primarily physical vehicle systems; scheduling control commands is not its core responsibility, causing high diffusion.
Directly responsible for remote command management and transmission; high cohesion and low diffusion.
Focuses on safety alerts and monitoring; scheduling control commands is tangential, causing moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the prioritized data scheduling system for control commands, as it directly manages remote command transmission and can encapsulate the scheduling logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related command prioritization is required.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability may affect scheduling effectiveness.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If scheduling must consider safety-critical prioritization, refine the DP to explicitly include coordination with Safety Services to improve containment and interface clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of establishing a prioritized data scheduling system directly addresses the FR's requirement to support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency ensuring safe remote operation. Prioritizing critical control commands logically supports meeting latency and safety goals.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related command prioritization is required.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module has high containment (9) and low diffusion (2) scores, indicating it is the most appropriate and cohesive boundary for implementing the prioritized data scheduling system. Other modules show higher diffusion and lower containment, supporting this allocation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be grounded in measuring the p95 latency of teleoperation commands under agreed network conditions, which is feasible given the Teleoperation module's control over scheduling and transmission.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The Teleoperation module clearly owns the scheduling responsibility with well-defined interfaces for receiving commands (Tasking) and sending prioritized commands (Network Link), providing clear allocation and ownership.

recommended_revisions: If scheduling must consider safety-critical prioritization, refine the DP to explicitly include coordination with Safety Services to improve containment and interface clarity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating the current DP and IM allocation are sound and verifiable. However, the high safety criticality and noted potential overlap with Safety Services suggest that further decomposition of the FR is warranted to explicitly address safety-critical prioritization coordination. This will improve containment, interface clarity, and reduce diffusion risks by refining the DP and IM boundaries accordingly.
next_level_sub_frs: Prioritize critical control commands for teleoperation latency
Coordinate scheduling with Safety Services for safety-critical commands
Manage non-critical data scheduling to optimize bandwidth

revise.why: To explicitly include coordination with Safety Services for safety-critical command prioritization, improving containment and interface clarity.

assumptions: The Teleoperation module is the primary owner of scheduling and transmission functions.
Safety Services module handles safety-critical command validation and enforcement.
open_risks: Potential overlap and diffusion between Teleoperation and Safety Services modules if coordination is not well defined.
evidence_needed: Verification data on p95 latency under prioritized scheduling.
Interface specifications between Teleoperation and Safety Services modules for command prioritization.
